description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the ubuntu Docker image. Add a global before_script that runs: which ssh-agent || ( apt-get update -y && apt-get install openssh-client git -y ), eval $(ssh-agent -s), mkdir -p ~/.ssh, chmod 700 ~/.ssh, echo 'echo $SSH_PASSPHRASE' > ~/.ssh/tmp && chmod 700 ~/.ssh/tmp, echo "$SSH_PRIVATE_KEY" | tr -d '\r' | DISPLAY=None SSH_ASKPASS=~/.ssh/tmp ssh-add -, ssh-keyscan gitlab.com >> ~/.ssh/known_hosts, chmod 644 ~/.ssh/known_hosts, git config --global user.email "darth@empire.com", and git config --global user.name "Darth Vader". Define a job named Test SSH in the default test stage that executes the script commands "ssh git@gitlab.com" and "git clone git@gitlab.com:gitlab-examples/ssh-private-key.git". The pipeline has no caches, artifacts, services, or conditional rules.
